query: I have a Riverpod TodoListNotifier holding a List<Todo>. Add an addTodo(Todo todo) method. The UI isn't rebuilding when I add items — fix that too.
expected_behavior: emits a NEW list via spread (`[...state, todo]`) or `.toList()`, producing a new reference each update
assigns the result back to `state` (Riverpod) so a `!=` value triggers a rebuild
the rendering widget uses `ref.watch(...)` inside `build`, not `ref.read`
ends with a Check: list of things to verify (per the output contract)
anti_behaviors: calls `state.add(todo)` / `state.items.add(...)` mutating the existing list then re-emits the same reference
uses `ref.read(provider)` inside `build` to render state
uses legacy `StateNotifierProvider` / `ChangeNotifierProvider`

query: In my flutter_bloc TodoBloc, removing a todo doesn't update the screen even though the handler runs. Here's the on<RemoveTodo> handler. Fix it.
expected_behavior: emits a NEW state instance containing a NEW collection, e.g. `emit(TodoLoaded(state.todos.where((t) => t.id != id).toList()))`
state class lists EVERY field in Equatable `props` (or uses freezed) so equality is correct
side effects (snackbars/navigation) go in `BlocListener`, not `BlocBuilder`
anti_behaviors: mutates `state.todos` in place (`.remove(...)`) then emits a state holding the same list reference
emits a state whose `props` omit the changed field, making the new state compare equal and the emit silently dropped

query: Set up a centralized Material 3 theme with light and dark mode for my app. Brand color is a purple around #6750A4.
expected_behavior: generates the scheme with `ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: ..., brightness: ...)` for both light and dark
defines `ThemeData` in a centralized location (e.g. `lib/core/theme/`), not inline per screen
wires `MaterialApp(theme:, darkTheme:, themeMode:)`
widgets read colors via `Theme.of(context).colorScheme` / `.textTheme`
anti_behaviors: hardcodes `Color(0xFF...)` hex literals inside widget build methods
uses deprecated `primaryColor` / `accentColor` / `backgroundColor` on ThemeData
sets `useMaterial3: false` or hand-picks every color instead of seeding

query: My screen shows 5000 messages in a SingleChildScrollView with a Column of message widgets and it's janky. Make it smooth.
expected_behavior: replaces the `Column` with `ListView.builder` (or `SliverList.builder`) so items build lazily
extracts each row as a `const`-capable `StatelessWidget` rather than a `_buildRow()` helper method
mentions stable `Key`s and/or `itemExtent` for fixed-height rows where applicable
anti_behaviors: keeps `SingleChildScrollView` + `Column(children: items.map(...).toList())` for the long list
builds all 5000 children up front via `.map().toList()`
uses a `Widget _buildRow()` helper method instead of a real widget class

query: Review this StatefulWidget that creates an AnimationController and a ScrollController. Is it leaking? Fix any leaks.
expected_behavior: disposes every controller (`AnimationController`, `ScrollController`) in `dispose()`
creates controllers in `initState`, not in `build`
checks `mounted` after any `await` before calling `setState` / touching `context`
anti_behaviors: leaves a controller undisposed
creates a controller inside `build()`
stores `BuildContext` in a long-lived field

query: Build a login form with email + password fields, validation, and an async submit that calls authRepo.login then navigates home on success.
expected_behavior: uses a `StatefulWidget` and disposes every `TextEditingController` (and `FocusNode`) in `dispose()`
guards the post-await context with `if (!context.mounted) return;` before navigating or showing a snackbar
follows a `validate()` → `save()` → submit flow and disables the submit button while loading
wires `textInputAction` / `onFieldSubmitted` for focus navigation between fields
anti_behaviors: creates `TextEditingController` inside `build` or never disposes it
uses `context` after `await` without a `context.mounted` check
leaves the submit button enabled during the async call (no double-tap guard)

query: My UserRepository.fetchUser does a network call and throws on failure, and my UI wraps it in try/catch. Refactor to typed error handling.
expected_behavior: returns a sealed `Result<T>` (Success | Failure) from the repository, not a thrown exception
maps the caught exception to a typed `AppFailure` (Server/Network/Timeout/...) at the data-layer boundary
each `Failure` carries a user-safe `message`; raw exceptions/stack traces are not shown to users
the UI pattern-matches the `Result` exhaustively (switch) instead of try/catch
anti_behaviors: leaves an empty `catch {}` or silent `catchError((_) {})` that swallows the error
keeps `try/catch` in the UI layer for the network call
shows the raw exception or stack trace to the user

query: Set up a dio-based API client for my app with a Bearer auth header and request logging. Base URL is https://api.example.com.
expected_behavior: builds ONE configured `Dio` instance (base URL, timeouts) injected via DI, not `Dio()` per request
adds the auth Bearer token in an interceptor `onRequest`, not inline at each call site
guards `LogInterceptor` with `kDebugMode` so bodies/tokens are not logged in release
reads the base URL from config / `--dart-define` / an `Env` object rather than committing a secret
anti_behaviors: constructs `Dio()` per request instead of reusing one instance
hardcodes an API key or secret token literal in the client source
enables `LogInterceptor` unconditionally (dumps auth headers in release)

query: Add go_router to my app with a /login route and a /home route gated behind auth, plus a bottom-nav shell. Deep links must keep working.
expected_behavior: configures ONE `GoRouter` with a `rootNavigatorKey` passed to `MaterialApp.router(routerConfig:)`
centralizes auth gating in the router `redirect` plus a `refreshListenable` tied to auth state
uses typed routes (`GoRouteData` + `@TypedGoRoute`) and navigates with route objects, not raw string paths
provides an `errorBuilder` for unmatched paths
anti_behaviors: scatters auth checks inside individual widgets instead of the router redirect
sets a fixed `initialLocation` that clobbers an incoming deep link
creates a second router / mixes Navigator 1.0 with go_router

query: Clean up this Dart class: it uses `var` everywhere, positional params, and a few `!` force-unwraps. Make it idiomatic.
expected_behavior: defaults fields and locals to `final`, using `var` only where a value is genuinely reassigned
uses `required` named parameters for mandatory inputs
replaces unjustified `!` with `?.` / `??` / null-promotion
names are intention-revealing (no abbreviations like `db`/`temp`)
anti_behaviors: keeps unnecessary `!` force-unwraps on values that aren't provably non-null
leaves `var` on fields that are never reassigned
uses bare `late` without a real need, or `dynamic` to dodge typing

query: Model a User entity with id, name, and optional email. It should have value equality and be usable in a Set.
expected_behavior: all fields are `final` (no setters) and the class has a `const` constructor where possible
implements structural `==` and `hashCode` (via `Object.hash`) or uses freezed to generate them
uses `required` for id and name; `String?` only for the genuinely-optional email
every field is typed (no `dynamic`)
anti_behaviors: uses mutable (non-final) fields or setters
relies on default reference equality (no `==`/`hashCode`) for a value object
marks a mandatory field nullable with a null default instead of `required`

query: This widget awaits fetchProfile(), then fetchSettings(), then fetchFeed() one after another, and subscribes to a notifications stream. Speed it up and stop the leak.
expected_behavior: runs the three independent fetches concurrently with `Future.wait` (or record `.wait`), not sequential awaits
cancels the `StreamSubscription` (and closes any controller) in `dispose`
handles errors on each async path (try/catch or typed failure), never a silent drop
re-checks `mounted` after an `await` before using state/`context`
anti_behaviors: keeps the three independent fetches as sequential `await`s
leaves the `StreamSubscription` uncancelled (leak)
drops a future fire-and-forget without `unawaited(...)` or error handling

query: Write a unit test for LoginUseCase, which depends on an AuthRepository. Cover the success path and the invalid-credentials failure path.
expected_behavior: mocks `AuthRepository` with `mocktail` (no codegen) and registers fallbacks if needed
tests follow Arrange-Act-Assert structure with clear setup, call, and assertion sections
asserts on behavior/returned value (and interactions via `verify`), not merely that it does not throw
covers both the success and the failure path as separate tests
anti_behaviors: asserts only `returnsNormally` / 'no throw' without checking the actual result
calls the real `AuthRepository` instead of a mock
merges arrange/act/assert into one tangled block with no structure

query: Review this diff: it adds a screen that hardcodes an API key, never disposes its AnimationController, and uses a SingleChildScrollView+Column for a 1000-item list.
expected_behavior: leads with a verdict (Changes needed / Approve) and counts of findings
groups findings by severity: Blocking, Should-fix, Nit
flags the hardcoded API key and the undisposed controller as Blocking (security + leak)
gives each finding a concrete fix and ends with a Definition-of-done check
anti_behaviors: silently rewrites the code instead of reporting findings (unless asked to apply fixes)
misclassifies the hardcoded secret or the leak as a Nit
returns prose with no severity grouping
